implications: Applicant CV: Lists multiple research grants (NIHR, UCLH) and formal training (Biostatistics, MRes module), demonstrating relevant research experience and training.
Applicant Research Background: Details extensive experience in quantitative/qualitative methods, trial delivery (SIPSMART2), and formal training (MRes, MSc).

Training & Development and Research Support: Details prior experience in quantitative research and statistics, alongside a plan to acquire specific qualitative and mixed-methods skills required for the project.
Applicant CV: Extensive publication record (10+ items) in peer-reviewed journals and awards (e.g., Best Abstract Prize, Dean's List) confirm impactful research outputs.
Applicant Research Background: Lists multiple publications in peer-reviewed journals, conference presentations, and awards (Dean's List, Best Abstract Prize).
Applicant CV: Career progression from Band 5 to Band 8a Clinical Lead and Associate Principal Investigator roles shows increasing responsibility and leadership trajectory.
Applicant Research Background: Demonstrates increasing responsibility through Associate-PI roles, supervising dissertations, and leading clinical teams.

Training & Development and Research Support: Explicitly states career ambitions to lead an ERAS Centre of Excellence and includes leadership training (Rosalind Franklin Programme) to support this trajectory.
Applicant CV: Current role as Senior Specialist Dietitian alongside research grants and publications demonstrates a strong commitment to a practitioner-academic career.
Applicant Research Background: Strong evidence of balancing full-time clinical work with research outputs, audits, and academic qualifications.

Training & Development and Research Support: Plans to maintain clinical practice (one day/fortnight) and shadow clinical teams, demonstrating a commitment to balancing practitioner and academic roles.
Applicant CV: High volume of recent publications and successful grant applications indicate a strong track record of previous research quality.
Applicant Research Background: High-quality prior research evidenced by publications in Clinical Nutrition and Oral Oncology Reports, plus successful grant awards.
Applicant CV: Secured a PPI starter-grant to form a patient group and conduct discussions, showing prior integration of lived experience in research design.
Applicant Research Background: Mentions securing a PPI starter-grant and incorporating patient feedback into research development.
Plain English Summary of Research: Demonstrates integration of lived experience by discussing the project with patients who identified lack of confidence and support as key issues.

Patient & Public Involvement: Details specific design changes (e.g., outcome measures, interview questions, staff inclusion) directly resulting from PPI feedback, strongly supporting integration of lived experience.

Applicant Research Background: Clearly states a research vision to improve nutritional care in HNC and a trajectory to become a clinical research leader.

Training & Development and Research Support: Clearly articulates a vision to become a Consultant Clinical Academic Dietitian leading a research group, with a detailed training plan supporting this trajectory.
Applicant Research Background: Explicitly identifies the fellowship as the 'crucial next step' to transition from a developing academic to a research leader.

Plain English Summary of Research: Identifies a knowledge gap regarding factors affecting the restart of eating/drinking and cites previous research statistics (40-72% insufficient intake).
Scientific Abstract: Identifies limited evidence on optimal practice and explicitly plans a scoping review to map knowledge gaps, supporting pr.2.

Detailed Research Plan: Critically analyzes existing literature, identifying specific gaps such as selection bias, non-validated measures, and lack of data on protein intake, strongly supporting pr.2.
Plain English Summary of Research: Describes a feasible scope involving a literature review, a survey across 65 hospitals, and data collection from records, appropriate for a fellowship project.

Detailed Research Plan: Defines a feasible 36-month scope with 5 workstreams, specific sample sizes (n=90), and realistic recruitment targets, supporting pr.3.
Plain English Summary of Research: Explicitly details a mixed-methods design: reviewing articles, surveying staff, analyzing hospital records, and observing practices to understand barriers.
Scientific Abstract: Details a mixed-methods convergent-parallel design with specific techniques (scoping review, survey, regression, framework analysis), supporting pr.4.
Detailed Research Plan: Provides explicit methodological details including Arksey & O'Malley framework, PG-SGA outcomes, and regression analysis plans, strongly supporting pr.4.

Detailed Research Plan: Quantifies patient benefits (reduced malnutrition, improved QoL) and economic impact (£90k-£13m savings), clearly supporting pr.5.
